evals.name: python-flask-orders-review
evals.prompt: Please do an SSEM-style securability review of this Python Flask order-handling module. It's part of a B2C customer portal. I want the full SSEM scorecard with sub-attribute scores, the three-part report, and a 45-item checklist. Be specific about what's broken — engineering improvements, not generic 'follow best practices' advice. The file is attached.

assertions.text: Output contains the three-part SSEM report (Score Summary / Detailed Findings / Appendix A checklist) with all three parts present and clearly labeled

Each of the nine sub-attributes (Analyzability, Modifiability, Testability, Confidentiality, Authenticity & Accountability, Integrity, Integrity Operational, Resilience, Availability) has a numeric 0-10 score
Sub-attribute scores show meaningful differentiation — not all the same number — reflecting the mixed quality of the code under review
Pillar weighted scores are computed and the math is shown (Maintainability 33%, Trustworthiness 34%, Reliability 33% with the prescribed sub-weights)
The overall score and a grade (Excellent/Good/Adequate/Fair/Poor) are produced consistent with the grading scale
SQL injection in get_order via string concatenation is flagged with a specific code reference and remediation
Hardcoded DB_PASSWORD secret is flagged with a specific code reference and remediation
create_order accepting client-supplied user_id (Derived Integrity violation) is flagged
Lack of structured logging (use of print() and limited audit fields) is flagged under Accountability
Lack of tests ("No tests in this module yet") is flagged under Testability
Recommendations include priority labels (High/Medium/Low) and concrete actionable steps with file/line references
Appendix A includes a 45-item checklist (15 each for Maintainability, Trustworthiness, Reliability) with pass/fail markers and a summary count

evals.name: node-express-tickets-review
evals.prompt: Run a securable-engineering review of this Express/TypeScript helpdesk-tickets file. Score it through SSEM, give me the three-part report, and call out the engineering work that needs to happen before we'd consider this audit-ready. File is attached.

assertions.text: Output contains the three-part SSEM report (Score Summary / Detailed Findings / Appendix A checklist) with all three parts present
Each of the nine sub-attributes has a numeric 0-10 score
Scores show meaningful differentiation across sub-attributes (not uniform)
Pillar weighted scores are computed with visible math; overall score and grade produced
Use of `any` typing on req and on the JWT payload (loss of type safety, possible payload-injection of arbitrary fields) is flagged
Mass-assignment risk in PATCH /tickets/:id (spreading req.body directly into the update) is flagged with remediation suggesting a field allowlist
DELETE endpoint lacks authorization check (any authenticated user can delete any ticket) is flagged
JWT verification accepts the token without pinning the algorithm — algorithm-confusion / alg:none risk — is flagged
Authorization filter for non-admins relies on `req.user.email` which the JWT payload may control (Derived Integrity / authz on client-asserted state) is flagged
Logging gaps and lack of structured audit logging on ticket lifecycle events are flagged under Accountability
Recommendations include priority labels and concrete actionable steps with file/line references
Appendix A includes a 45-item checklist (15 each pillar) with pass/fail markers and a summary count

evals.name: go-http-files-review
evals.prompt: I need an SSEM securability review on this Go file-handling service. Score every sub-attribute, give me the three-part report, and prioritize the issues so I know what to fix first. File is attached.

assertions.text: Output contains the three-part SSEM report (Score Summary / Detailed Findings / Appendix A checklist) with all three parts present
Each of the nine sub-attributes has a numeric 0-10 score
Scores show meaningful differentiation across sub-attributes
Pillar weighted scores are computed with visible math; overall score and grade produced
Path traversal vulnerability in HandleUpload/HandleDownload (filename taken from query string, joined into path with no traversal sanitization) is flagged with specific remediation
Tenant identity taken from a request header (X-Tenant-ID) without authentication or signed-claim verification is flagged
Reading the entire upload body with ioutil.ReadAll (no size limit, full buffer in memory) is flagged under Availability/Resilience
Use of deprecated ioutil package (and missing context propagation / timeouts) is flagged
Errors propagated raw to the response body via http.Error(w, err.Error(), ...) leaking internal paths is flagged
Lack of structured logging (use of fmt.Println) is flagged under Accountability
Recommendations include priority labels and concrete actionable steps with file/line references
Appendix A includes a 45-item checklist (15 each pillar) with pass/fail markers and a summary count
